commands.review_summary:
The branch adds snake_case OpenClaw diagnostic identifier keys to the diagnostics OTel redaction drop list, adds focused log-attribute redaction coverage, and adds a changelog entry.

Reproducibility: yes. by source inspection: current main maps log.record attributes like session_id to openclaw.session_id, then redacts only the camelCase id keys. I did not execute an exporter run, so this is source-reproducible rather than executed proof.
commands.review_followup: Contributor or maintainer action is needed for real behavior proof and the telemetry compatibility decision; an automated repair lane cannot supply the contributor's runtime proof.

target.title: fix(diagnostics): drop snake case OTel ids
target.body:
## Summary
- Drop snake_case OpenClaw diagnostic identifiers from diagnostics-otel exported log attributes, matching the existing camelCase redaction behavior.
- Cover the behavior through the log export path so unrelated attributes, such as provider identity, still export.
- Add a changelog fix entry for the exported telemetry cleanup.

## Verification
- autoreview: `AUTOREVIEW_AUTO_TESTS=0 AUTOREVIEW_OPENCLAW_MAINTAINER_VALIDATION=1 .agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main` -> clean, patch correct 0.88 after rebase.
- fresh PR remote proof: AWS Crabbox `run_4faad2ff2b19`, lease `cbx_d8a5df47a772`, ran `pnpm test:serial extensions/diagnostics-otel/src/service.test.ts` and `pnpm check:changed` against openclaw/openclaw#72645 -> passed.
- focused test result: `extensions/diagnostics-otel/src/service.test.ts` passed 61 tests.

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: diagnostics-otel exported log attributes could include snake_case OpenClaw diagnostic IDs even though camelCase variants were already removed.
Real environment tested: AWS Crabbox c7a.8xlarge fresh PR checkout for openclaw/openclaw#72645, using the diagnostics-otel exporter path.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: In the fresh PR checkout, emitted a diagnostics-otel log record containing camelCase and snake_case diagnostic identifiers, then inspected the exported OTLP log attributes.
Evidence after fix: Terminal output from run_4faad2ff2b19 showed the exported OTLP log attributes preserved `openclaw.provider = test-provider` while `openclaw.call_id`, `openclaw.callId`, `openclaw.session_id`, and `openclaw.sessionId` were absent.
Observed result after fix: The exported log payload kept the operator-useful provider attribute and omitted both diagnostic identifier naming forms, so session/call/span IDs are no longer mirrored into exported log attributes.
What was not tested: A live collector deployment was not started; the fresh PR checkout exercised the diagnostics-otel export path directly and inspected the emitted OTLP payload.
target.author: lion0710
